description: Integer 0 encoded as UInt8 (40 00) instead of TinyUInt (13). Writers MUST use TinyUInt; readers SHOULD accept the non-minimal form (§11.1: 'Non-minimal integer encoding — SHOULD accept').

String "a" encoded with the generic 0x6F tag (6F 01 61) instead of TinyString (60 61). Writers MUST use TinyString for 1..15 bytes; readers SHOULD accept the generic form (§11.1: 'Non-minimal string encoding — SHOULD accept').
Empty array encoded as an n=0 array leaf (70 00) instead of the EmptyArray singleton (33). Writers MUST emit the singleton (§7.0); readers SHOULD accept the n=0 leaf (§11.1).

Single-element array [1] encoded with offset width w=1 (tag 0x71, two-byte slots) when w=0 would have fit. Writers MUST pick the smallest fitting W; readers SHOULD accept any W that fits (§7, §11.1: 'Non-minimal offset width — SHOULD accept').

Extension type code 0xC0 with varuint length 3 and payload bytes 'abc'. Writers MUST NOT emit Extension codes (§4); readers SHOULD treat them as opaque using the length prefix to skip or surface bytes. No JSON correspondence — Extension codes are reserved for future spec versions.
